questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh was which numbered Guru of the Sikhs?
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Singh was the tenth Sikh Guru.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh founded which order in 1699?
questions.explanation: He founded the Khalsa in 1699 CE.

questions.q: In which years did Guru Gobind Singh lead the Sikh community?
questions.explanation: He led the Sikhs in the late 17th and early 18th centuries CE.

questions.q: Besides being a spiritual leader, Guru Gobind Singh was also known as a what?
questions.explanation: He was a warrior, poet, and spiritual leader.

questions.q: The Khalsa founded by Guru Gobind Singh shaped which identity of the Sikh tradition?
questions.explanation: The Khalsa shaped the martial and religious identity of the Sikhs.

questions.q: In which year was the Khalsa established?
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Singh established the Khalsa in 1699 CE.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh was a leader of which religious community?
questions.explanation: He led the Sikh community as its tenth Guru.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh's surname 'Singh' came to mean what among the Khalsa?
questions.explanation: Singh means lion, a name adopted by initiated Khalsa men.

questions.q: Members of the Khalsa initiated by Guru Gobind Singh underwent which ceremony?
questions.explanation: Khalsa initiation was the Amrit ceremony (Khande di Pahul).

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh was the son of which earlier Sikh Guru?
questions.explanation: He was the son of the ninth Guru, Guru Tegh Bahadur.

questions.q: Where and in what year was Guru Gobind Singh born?
questions.explanation: He was born at Patna (Patna Sahib) in 1666 CE.

questions.q: The five beloved ones first initiated into the Khalsa are known by what name?
questions.explanation: The first five Khalsa initiates are called the Panj Pyare (Five Beloved Ones).

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh declared which scripture to be the eternal Guru after him?
questions.explanation: He proclaimed the Guru Granth Sahib as the eternal Guru, ending the line of human Gurus.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor was on the throne for most of Guru Gobind Singh's conflicts?
questions.explanation: He came into conflict chiefly with the forces of the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb.

questions.q: Which town was a key center for Guru Gobind Singh, where he founded the Khalsa?
questions.explanation: He founded the Khalsa at Anandpur Sahib in the Punjab hills.

questions.q: The four sons of Guru Gobind Singh are collectively remembered as the what?
questions.explanation: His four sons are revered together as the Char Sahibzade (four princes).

questions.q: The five articles of faith worn by initiated Khalsa Sikhs are commonly called the what?
questions.explanation: The Khalsa code prescribes the Five Ks (kesh, kangha, kara, kachera, kirpan).

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh's father, Guru Tegh Bahadur, was executed on the orders of which emperor?
questions.explanation: Guru Tegh Bahadur was executed in Delhi on Aurangzeb's orders in 1675.

questions.q: In which year did Guru Gobind Singh die?
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Singh died in 1708 CE at Nanded.

questions.q: At which southern town did Guru Gobind Singh spend his final days and pass away?
questions.explanation: He spent his last days and died at Nanded in present-day Maharashtra in 1708.

questions.q: Which letter did Guru Gobind Singh address to the emperor Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: He wrote the Zafarnama (Epistle of Victory) in Persian verse to Aurangzeb.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh's military encounters were fought mostly against the Mughals and which allied power?
questions.explanation: He fought both Mughal forces and the allied hill rajas of the Punjab foothills.

questions.q: At which battle did Guru Gobind Singh fight in December 1704 after evacuating Anandpur?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Chamkaur (December 1704) was fought after the Sikhs left Anandpur Sahib.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh's two younger sons were famously bricked up alive at which place?
questions.explanation: His two youngest sons were executed at Sirhind by the Mughal governor Wazir Khan.

questions.q: The Dasam Granth is a body of writings traditionally attributed to whom?
questions.explanation: The Dasam Granth is traditionally attributed to the tenth Guru, Gobind Singh.

questions.q: What was Guru Gobind Singh's name before he assumed the title of Guru?
questions.explanation: He was born and known as Gobind Rai before becoming Guru and later Gobind Singh.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh's first major battle, fought in 1688 against allied hill rajas, was the Battle of what?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Bhangani (1688) was his first major military engagement.

questions.q: Before his death, Guru Gobind Singh commissioned which leader to carry on the struggle in the Punjab?
questions.explanation: He sent Banda Singh Bahadur to the Punjab to continue the campaign against Mughal authorities.

questions.q: The salutation 'Waheguru Ji Ka Khalsa, Waheguru Ji Ki Fateh' is associated with the order founded by whom?
questions.explanation: This Khalsa salutation is rooted in the order Guru Gobind Singh created in 1699.

questions.q: Guru Gobind Singh established his final preaching seat (takht) at Nanded, known today as which?
questions.explanation: Takht Sri Hazur Sahib at Nanded marks the place of his final days and passing.
